source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The diagram shows the eight vertices of an octagon connected by line segments. Jodhvir wants to write one of the integers 1,2,3 or 4 at each of the vertices so that the two integers at the ends of every line segment are different. He has already written three integers as shown.
<image1>
How many times will the integer 4 appear in his completed diagram?

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to a diagram and to three integers already written 'as shown', so the exact setup is not recoverable from text alone.
The task depends on the line-segment connections among octagon vertices, which are visual/structural information in the image.
multi_step: Solving requires assigning numbers to multiple vertices under edge-based inequality constraints, not a single direct lookup.
The question combines diagram interpretation with constraint reasoning and then asks for a count of how many times the integer 4 appears.
There are eight vertices and some values are prefilled, suggesting propagation across several dependent placements.
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('4'), which is easy to compare against a verified solution.
registry_passed=true and the image asset exists, so the full problem statement appears available for checking.
The constraint-based nature of the task makes it amenable to rule-based or solver-based verification once the graph and given labels are read from the image.

decision_reasons: Image, question text, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are available.
Asset registry passed with no recorded issues.
High verifiability score and numeric answer make the sample straightforward to check once the diagram is read.
Although the problem is strongly diagram-dependent, the required image asset exists, so this is not an asset-gap case.
